item.title: mark_item_failed silently swallows update errors with || true
item.intent: Sprint failure context must be recorded reliably — if mark_item_failed silently does nothing, subsequent retries and auto mode have no failure history to work from.
item.description: In mark_item_failed(), the call to update_item() is followed by '2>/dev/null || true', which suppresses all errors and always succeeds. If the backlog file write fails (bad JSON, permissions, missing file), the item is never marked failed and no error is surfaced. Auto mode and retry logic read failCount and lastFailReason from the item — if mark_item_failed silently no-ops, those fields are never set, degrading sprint recovery. Fix: remove || true, keep stderr suppression only when source_file is empty (item lookup already failed), and log a warning if update_item returns non-zero.
steps: Locate the update_item call with '2>/dev/null || true'
Split the logic: if source_file is empty (item not found), it is expected to fail — log_warn and return 0

Otherwise, call update_item without || true; on failure, emit log_warn 'Failed to record sprint failure for $item_id' but still return 0 (best-effort — do not abort the sprint just because the metadata write failed)
Verify: mark_item_failed on a known item updates failCount; mark_item_failed on an unknown ID logs a warning
acceptanceCriteria: mark_item_failed logs a warning when update_item fails instead of silently succeeding
mark_item_failed on a known item correctly sets failCount and lastFailReason
Sprint execution continues even if mark_item_failed cannot write (best-effort semantics preserved)
